name: @chbrain/khai-engine-secret
description: khai engine content, the secret domain: a withheld fact that carries more weight than it shows -- what the keeper holds back, binds by, and pays to keep.
license: SEE LICENSE IN LICENSE and LICENSE-CODE

khai.tagline: Secret as piece: a withheld fact that carries more weight than it shows -- the thing one persona knows and another does not, the work of keeping it, the choice of telling it, and the moment it gets out.

card.wire: Attaches at Piece. The anchor (piece_secret.md) declares the secret as what it is in the room: a fact one persona holds and another lacks, worth more than its surface, and never neutral once it exists. The law makes the asymmetry of knowledge a thing the scene can carry.
card.issue: The secret, read four ways beneath the fact. One position names who carries it (the keeper); three processes name what happens around it -- the work of holding it back (concealment), the deliberate telling (disclosure), and the moment it gets out against the keeper's will (exposure). The fact is the piece; the members are the pressure it exerts.
card.require: Two altitudes hold the engine. The law: declared once in the project's Instructions Knowledge chapter, linking the anchor, so a secret is read as load-bearing wherever it sits. The link: the persona who holds it links the keeper stance and the process it is in under Projection.
card.enforce: Structure is checked; coherence is judged. The kit verifies the floor: the law is declared and a real keeper position or secret process is linked. Whether the secret actually bears the weight the scene needs is semantic, taught and reviewed, never failed by the suite.
card.setup: Declare the law in the Instructions Knowledge chapter, linking the anchor. Then place the secret: name the fact, who holds it and who does not, and which process it is in -- being concealed, about to be disclosed, or breaking into exposure. Read it in what the keeper will not say and the cost of not saying it.
